# GitHub Repository Setup Guide

After pushing ShipyardMD to GitHub, apply these settings to maximize discoverability.

---

## Repository Description (copy-paste this)

```
AI-powered development framework for Claude Code. Start any project — iOS, web SaaS, e-commerce, Telegram bot — with clean architecture, security defaults, and production-ready infrastructure. One command install.
```

---

## Topics / Tags (add all of these in GitHub Settings → Topics)

```
claude-code
claude-ai
anthropic
ai-framework
developer-tools
developer-productivity
project-template
boilerplate
swift
swiftui
ios-development
laravel
php
nextjs
react
docker
clean-architecture
mobile-development
web-development
saas-starter
saas-boilerplate
fullstack
devops
ci-cd
security
open-source
```

How to add: GitHub repo page → gear icon next to "About" → Topics → paste each one

---

## Repository Settings

- **Visibility**: Public
- **Features to enable**: Issues ✓, Wiki ✗ (use docs/ instead), Discussions ✓
- **Default branch**: `main`
- **Social preview image**: Create a 1280×640px banner (use Figma or Canva)
  - Background: dark (#0d1117)
  - Title: "ShipyardMD" large, bold
  - Subtitle: "AI-powered dev framework for Claude Code"
  - Show the terminal install command

---

## After First Push — Pin to Profile

1. Go to your GitHub profile
2. Click "Customize your pins"
3. Pin `shipyardMD` repo

---

## Get Initial Stars

Share in these communities once live:
- Reddit: r/ClaudeAI, r/artificial, r/webdev, r/iOSProgramming
- X/Twitter: tag @AnthropicAI and use #ClaudeCode #DevTools
- Hacker News: "Show HN: ShipyardMD — AI dev framework for Claude Code"
- Product Hunt: schedule a launch for a Tuesday/Wednesday 12:01am PT
- Discord: Anthropic official Discord, iOS devs Discord
